title: Resource APIs, Idempotency, Concurrency, Pagination, and Consistency · Contract Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for resource apis, idempotency, concurrency, pagination, and consistency and demands the smallest safe consistent resource API and operation ledger with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for consistent resource API and operation ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model resource states, legal transitions, commands, representations, invariants, tombstones, and conflict outcomes independently of table layout.
Scope idempotency keys to principal and operation, compare canonical request fingerprints, store outcome atomically, expire deliberately, and reject mismatches.
Connect version columns, ETags, preconditions, atomic updates, affected-row checks, conflict disclosure, and safe retry behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resource State Machine
steps.instruction:
Order the Resource State Machine Node and Express server HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resource State Machine.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Resource State Machine assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Node and Express server Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Resource State Machine, keep Resource State Machine connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz team handles case 1021 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to join idempotency fingerprints, optimistic concurrency, and transactional outbox evidence; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model resource states, legal transitions, commands, representations, invariants, tombstones, and conflict outcomes independently of table layout.

content.text: Order work so Resource State Machine has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
